summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: Ramp elevates CTO to co-CEO
articles.description: “If you know us, this won’t feel like a change,” wrote Eric Glyman of fellow co-founder Karim Atiyeh’s move to the top of the fintech.

articles.title: Card networks, banks, fintechs partner on ‘low-cost’ stablecoin
articles.description: Visa, Mastercard, Klarna and MoneyGram were among the companies that lent their backing to a digital asset launched Tuesday by Open Standard.

articles.title: Klarna wins $2B Google antitrust case
articles.description: A Swedish court ruled Wednesday that the tech giant had violated antitrust laws in price comparison, harming a Klarna-owned European competitor.

articles.title: Amazon settles FTC card fraud case
articles.description: Regulators allege the digital retail giant created a “Kafkaesque ordeal” for ID theft victims trying to obtain records related to fraudulent transactions.

articles.title: Payments events still ahead in 2026
articles.description: Stablecoins, fraud prevenetion and artificial intelligence have been hot topics at this year’s payments industry conferences. There’s more to come.

articles.title: Repay attracts new $1.4B bid
articles.description: Forager Capital Management boosted its offer to buy Repay Holdings, which said it plans to review the new bid.

articles.title: Rhode Island enacts self-checkout law
articles.description: Legislation signed by Gov. Dan McKee and due to take effect in 2027 comes as other states also consider self-checkout restrictions.

articles.title: SNAP payment errors too high: USDA
articles.description: A majority of states made inaccurate payments to beneficiaries in fiscal 2025 and could be required to pay part of the cost of benefits starting in 2027.
